# Questions in Livestream 43

Live Stream #43: Discovering the Rediscovered Exodus.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=239" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:59</a> | What is the ancient Near Eastern context for the Sabbath, and should the church still practice it? | He treated Sabbath as a radical departure from Egypt's labor rhythm and said its rest principle still matters, even if Christians are not bound to one sacred weekday. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=669" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:09</a> | What does he think of Bietak's later twelfth-century date for the Exodus? | He called it close but a little too late, especially in light of the Merneptah evidence for Israel already being in Canaan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=830" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:50</a> | Does the Bible imply a different ontology of reality than modern science? | He said the Bible assumes a world where divine and spiritual realities are real, while warning that naive naturalism is also philosophically inadequate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=1112" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:32</a> | If New Kingdom Canaan was under Egyptian control, how could Israel have entered it? | Falk said Egyptian control was a loose hegemony with weak garrisons, and that by the late Ramesside period Egypt was already losing real control. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=1246" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:46</a> | If Ramesses II lost so many chariots at the sea, should there be evidence of attacks on Egypt afterward? | He said yes in indirect form: Merneptah's struggles show Egypt was short on chariot strength soon after. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=1735" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:55</a> | Is there any meaningful comparison between the Egyptian Book of the Dead, the Tibetan Book of the Dead, and Jesus Christ? | He rejected the common Osiris-Jesus comparison and said Osiris was not resurrected in the same way Christ was. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=1983" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:03</a> | Did Egyptians ever debate or preserve memory of Passover-like events? | Not the biblical Passover itself, but Falk said Egyptian texts do mention plague-like and exodus-like events from other periods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=2230" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:10</a> | What marks the beginning and end of a clan? | He described clans as lineage units that persist through living succession and can split into new clans as branches mature. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=2486" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:26</a> | How does he understand the mixed multitude that left Egypt? | He treated it as a real multicultural group from places like Avaris that helped form Israel in the Sinai rather than a fully formed nation existing beforehand. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=2858" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:38</a> | Did Moses' fear of being God's spokesman imply that he had a speech impediment, and was that partly why God chose him? | Falk said Moses was "slow of speech," which may mean a speech impediment or slow verbal formulation; God may have used that weakness, but Moses' whole life preparation also mattered. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=3230" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:50</a> | Why is lex talionis so misrepresented as vengeance or retaliation? | He said it comes from clan politics and functioned as a limit on retaliation, preventing an injury from escalating into attacks on whole families or clans. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=3604" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:04</a> | Were the Israelites enslaved in Egypt for less than 400 years, perhaps around 215 years? | He agreed the stay was under 400 years, treated 215 as a possible minimum and about 350 as a maximum, and leaned closer to the higher end while warning against overreading the data. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=4080" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:00</a> | Was Akhenaten exiled in Sinai, and what might thorough excavations of Sinai find? | He rejected Akhenaten's exile in Sinai as fringe and unsupported; Sinai excavations might find mining camps, fortresses, seasonal sites, and sparse Bedouin remains, but conditions limit work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=4532" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:32</a> | Where does he agree and disagree with Michael Heiser's divine council interpretation? | He partly agreed for Iron Age texts such as Job and much of Psalm 82, but warned that applying the same lens to earlier material can create anachronisms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=4680" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:00</a> | Did Jonah really die in the belly of the fish and go to Sheol, or was he alive for three days? | He said Jonah truly died and was raised, making the sign of Jonah a strong parallel to Jesus' death and resurrection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=5016" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:36</a> | Are the Anunnaki Egyptian, and is the ankh connected to them? | No. He said the Anunnaki come from Mesopotamian literature, Egyptian primordial gods are only loosely comparable, and the ankh is not etymologically related. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=5572" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:52</a> | What are his feelings on Revelation, and why do people say it was not in the Bible until the nineteenth century? | He said that overstates the issue: Revelation existed, but the KJV Greek base involved back-translation from Latin, while stronger Greek manuscript evidence became available later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=5881" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:01</a> | What was Terah's ethnicity, language, and religion if he is identified with Terah of Urkesh, and are Abram or his other sons referenced? | Falk identified him as Mesopotamian, possibly Amorite, using Akkadian; one son may be referenced, Abraham is not named, and lunar deity worship is likely but uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=6272" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:44:32</a> | Why does he suppose Digital Hammurabi is unwilling to debate him? | Falk speculated that Digital Hammurabi prefers weaker debate opponents, but also said he himself is not especially interested in debates and prefers making new content. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=6446" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:26</a> | Is Jebel Musa really Mount Sinai? | It is one candidate among many, but he said we cannot identify Sinai with certainty at present. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=6761" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:41</a> | Why did war break out in heaven? | He said Revelation presents a rebellion but does not fully explain the motive, and he leaned toward pride and internal corruption rather than a simple reaction to salvation history. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=6902" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:02</a> | Is it a straw man to call the Israelites undeveloped Bronze Age goat herders? | Yes, or at least a heavily loaded caricature; Falk stressed that some Levantine groups were literate, skilled, and more complex than that slogan suggests. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=7191" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:51</a> | What does he think about the upcoming Bart Ehrman and Mike Licona debate? | He expected more heat than light, said it was outside his main interests, and probably would not spend time watching it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=7283" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:23</a> | If Thebes was still the capital, what was Pharaoh's daughter doing at Avaris when Moses was adopted? | Falk said the "daughter of Pharaoh" language is retrojective, tied to later elevation in status, while Moses was raised in the governor's royal household at Avaris. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=7628" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:08</a> | What happened to his project on interpreting prophecy? | It became a conference paper rather than a blog post, and a preliminary version was already available through his academic page. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=7902" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:42</a> | What is his favorite artifact from Tutankhamun's tomb? | He especially highlighted the small golden shrine, with the meteoritic iron dagger as a close second. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/l9Wg3VQCv-E?t=8218" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:58</a> | Where do the hieroglyphs in his background image come from? | From one of his own photos of a Fourth Dynasty mastaba on the Giza Plateau. |
